metadata.scheme_name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Free Education upto 12th Std. to All

raw_data.details: The scheme “Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Free Education upto 12th Std. to All” by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, is designed to support students from Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities by offering free education up to the 12th standard. The tuition fees for eligible students are waived, and the government reimburses the amount, ensuring financial barriers do not hinder access to education.
raw_data.benefits: Reimbursement: Full reimbursement of tuition fees for students up to the 12th standard.
raw_data.eligibility: The beneficiary should be a Student. The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu. The beneficiary should belong to the Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar converted to Christianity community. The beneficiary must be enrolled in a school up to the 12th standard.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineStudents/ Guardians should contact the Headmaster of the School with all the required documents as directed by the authority.
raw_data.documents_required: Proof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority). Caste certificate Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity community). Income certificate (if applicable). Proof of Admission. Any other documents (if required).

To know more please visit Check EligibilityAdi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Free Education upto 12th Std. to AllTamil NaduAdi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Free Education upto 12th Std. to AllAdi DravidarFree EducationScholarshipTribal WelfareCheck EligibilityDetailsThe scheme “Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Free Education upto 12th Std. to All” by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, is designed to support students from Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities by offering free education up to the 12th standard. The tuition fees for eligible students are waived, and the government reimburses the amount, ensuring financial barriers do not hinder access to education. BenefitsReimbursement: Full reimbursement of tuition fees for students up to the 12th standard. EligibilityThe beneficiary should be a Student. The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu. The beneficiary should belong to the Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar converted to Christianity community. The beneficiary must be enrolled in a school up to the 12th standard. Application ProcessOfflineStudents/ Guardians should contact the Headmaster of the School with all the required documents as directed by the authority. Documents RequiredProof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority). Caste certificate Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity community). Income certificate (if applicable). Proof of Admission. Any other documents (if required). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the purpose of the scheme? This scheme provides free education for Adi Dravidar, Tribal and Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity students up to 12th standard, covering tuition fees, which are reimbursed by the government.

Who is eligible for the scholarship? Students from the Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities are eligible.

What expenses are covered under this scheme? The scheme covers the full reimbursement of tuition fees up to the 12th standard.

Is there an income limit for eligibility? No, there is no income limit for eligibility under this scheme.

Up to which class does the scheme provide free education? The scheme provides free education up to the 12th standard.

What documents are required to apply? A caste certificate and proof of school enrollment are the key documents required.

Where should the application be submitted? The Headmaster of the school is responsible for submitting the application on behalf of the student.

Can students apply directly to the government? No, students cannot apply directly. The application is processed through the school. How often must students apply? The application process is usually handled annually or at the time of admission by the school.

Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.tn.gov.in/scheme/data_view/83084)OkWas this helpful?
